  let lens' := (xs.foldl step [[]] |>.map List.reverse).filter (fun l => l.Pairwise (· < ·)) |>.map List.length
  
  -- Prove lens lists sets are equal
  have h_lens_eq : ∀ n, n ∈ lens ↔ n ∈ lens' := by
    intro n; simp [lens, lens', step]
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨s, hs, hi, rfl⟩; use s; rw [h_subs] at hs; rw [h_incr] at hi
      constructor
      · rw [h_fold]; use s.reverse; simp; exact hs
      · exact ⟨hi, rfl⟩
    · rintro ⟨s, ⟨s', hs', rfl⟩, hi, rfl⟩
      use s; rw [h_subs]; rw [h_fold] at hs'; rcases hs' with ⟨u, hu, rfl⟩; simp at *
      exact ⟨hu, by rwa [←h_incr]⟩

  -- Analyze result = foldl max 0 lens
  let result := lens.foldl max 0
  have h0 : 0 ∈ lens := by simp [lens, subsequences, isStrictlyIncreasing]; use []
  
  -- Proving max properties manually to avoid library dependencies
  have h_max : result ∈ lens ∧ ∀ x ∈ lens, x ≤ result := by
    have gen : ∀ l z, (l.foldl max z = z ∨ l.foldl max z ∈ l) ∧ z ≤ l.foldl max z ∧ ∀ x ∈ l, x ≤ l.foldl max z := by
      intro l; induction l with
      | nil => simp
      | cons a l ih =>
        intro z; simp; rcases ih (max z a) with ⟨H1, H2, H3⟩
        refine ⟨?_, le_trans (le_max_left _ _) H2, ?_⟩
        · cases H1
          · rw [h]; by_cases hza : z ≤ a
            · right; left; simp [max_eq_right hza]
            · left; simp [max_eq_left (le_of_not_le hza)]
          · right; right; assumption
        · intro x hx; cases hx
          · trans (max z a); apply le_max_right; exact H2
          · apply H3; assumption
    rcases gen lens 0 with ⟨g1, g2, g3⟩
    constructor
    · cases g1; subst h; exact h0; assumption
    · exact g3
    
  rw [←h_lens_eq] at *
  exact ⟨h_max.1, fun _ h => h_max.2 _ (h_lens_eq _ |>.mpr h)⟩

have h_strict : ∀ l, isStrictlyIncreasing l ↔ l.Pairwise (· < ·) := by
    intro l; induction l <;> simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]
    next x xs ih => cases xs <;> simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]; next y ys =>
      split <;> simp [List.pairwise_cons, ih]
      next h => constructor <;> intro H <;> [refine ⟨fun z hz => Int.lt_trans h (List.rel_of_sorted_cons H _ hz), H⟩; exact H.2]
      next h => intro H; exact h (H.1 _ (List.mem_cons_self _ _))
  have h_subs : ∀ l, l ∈ subsequences xs ↔ l <+ xs := by
    induction xs <;> simp [subsequences]
    case nil => intro; constructor <;> intro h <;> try cases h; apply List.Sublist.slnil; constructor
    case cons x xs ih => intro l; constructor <;> intro h
      · rcases h with (h|⟨s,hs,rfl⟩); exact h.cons _; exact (ih.1 hs).cons2 _
      · cases h; exact Or.inl (ih.2 ‹_›); exact Or.inr ⟨_, ih.2 ‹_›, rfl⟩; exact Or.inl (ih.2 List.Sublist.slnil)
  let allSubseq := (xs.foldl (fun acc x => acc ++ acc.map (fun sub => x :: sub)) [[]]).map List.reverse
  have h_all : ∀ l, l ∈ allSubseq ↔ l <+ xs := by
    let step := fun acc (x : Int) => acc ++ acc.map (fun sub => x :: sub)
    have : ∀ acc l, l ∈ xs.foldl step acc ↔ ∃ a ∈ acc, ∃ s <+ xs, l = s.reverse ++ a := by
      intro acc; induction xs generalizing acc <;> simp [step, *]
      case nil => intro; constructor; exact fun h => ⟨_, h, [], .slnil, by simp⟩; rintro ⟨_,_,s,h,_⟩; cases h; simp [*]
      case cons => intro; constructor
        · rintro ⟨_,(h|⟨_,h,rfl⟩),s,hs,rfl⟩; exact ⟨_,h,s,hs.cons _,rfl⟩; exact ⟨_,h,x::s,hs.cons2 _,by simp⟩
        · rintro ⟨a,h,t,ht,rfl⟩; cases ht; exact ⟨_,Or.inl ⟨_,h,_,‹_›,rfl⟩⟩; exact ⟨_,Or.inr ⟨_,h,rfl⟩,_,‹_›,by simp⟩; exact ⟨_,Or.inl ⟨_,h,_,.slnil,rfl⟩⟩
    intro l; simp [List.mem_map]; constructor
    · rintro ⟨_, h, rfl⟩; rcases (this _ _).1 h with ⟨_,(rfl|⟨⟩),s,hs,rfl⟩; simp [hs]
    · intro h; refine ⟨l.reverse, (this _ _).2 ⟨[],Or.inl rfl, l, h, by simp⟩, by simp⟩
  have h_perm : ∀ n, n ∈ (allSubseq.filter (·.Pairwise (· < ·))).map List.length ↔ n ∈ L := by
    intro; simp [L, List.mem_map, List.mem_filter, h_strict, h_subs, h_all]; tauto
  have h_calc : L.foldl max 0 = L.foldr max 0 := by
    have : ∀ l a, l.foldl max a = max a (l.foldr max 0) := by
      intro l; induction l <;> simp [*, max_assoc, max_comm, max_left_comm]
    rw [this, max_comm, Nat.max_eq_right (Nat.zero_le _)]
  rw [List.foldl_map, h_calc, Bool.coe_and_iff, decide_eq_true_iff, decide_eq_true_iff, List.all_iff_forall]
  let M := L.foldr max 0
  have h_le : ∀ x ∈ L, x ≤ M := by intro x h; induction L <;> simp at h ⊢; cases h <;> [subst x; apply Nat.le_max_left; apply Nat.le_trans (‹_› ‹_›) (Nat.le_max_right _ _)]
  have h_mem : M ∈ L := by
    have : 0 ∈ L := by simp [L, h_subs, h_strict]; use []; exact ⟨.slnil, .nil⟩
    have : M ∈ 0 :: L := by induction L <;> simp [max_choice, *]; tauto
    cases this <;> [subst M; assumption]
  constructor
  · rw [h_perm]; exact h_mem
  · intro x hx; rw [h_perm] at hx; exact h_le x hx

-- 1. Equivalence of subsequences generation
  have mem_subsequences {α} (l : List α) (L : List α) : l ∈ subsequences L ↔ l <+ L := by
    induction L generalizing l with
    | nil => simp [subsequences]; intro h; rw [h]; constructor
    | cons x xs ih =>
      simp [subsequences]
      constructor
      · rintro (h | ⟨k, hk, rfl⟩)
        · exact List.Sublist.cons _ (ih _ |>.mp h)
        · exact List.Sublist.cons2 _ (ih _ |>.mp hk)
      · intro h
        cases h with
        | cons _ h => left; exact ih _ |>.mpr h
        | cons2 _ h => right; use l.tail; simp; exact ih _ |>.mpr h

  -- 2. Equivalence of strictly increasing check
  have strict_iff_pairwise (l : List Int) : isStrictlyIncreasing l ↔ l.Pairwise (· < ·) := by
    induction l with
    | nil => simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]
    | cons x xs ih =>
      cases xs with
      | nil => simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]
      | cons y ys =>
        simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]
        split
        next hlt =>
          rw [ih]
          constructor
          · intro h; constructor
            · intro z hz; apply lt_trans hlt; exact List.rel_of_pairwise_cons h hz
            · exact h
          · intro h; exact h.tail
        next hge =>
          simp at hge; constructor
          · intro h; have := List.rel_of_pairwise_cons h (List.mem_cons_self y ys); contradiction
          · intro h; exact h.tail

  -- 3. Connect postcondition construction to subsequences
  let P (L : List Int) := L.foldl (fun acc x => acc ++ acc.map (fun sub => x :: sub)) [[]]
  have P_eq_subseqs_rev (L : List Int) : P L = subsequences L.reverse := by
    induction L using List.reverseRecOn with
    | nil => rfl
    | append_singleton ys y ih =>
      simp [P, List.foldl_append, ih, subsequences, List.map_map]

  let allSubseq := P xs |>.map List.reverse
  have mem_post_iff (l : List Int) : l ∈ allSubseq ↔ l ∈ subsequences xs := by
    simp [allSubseq, P_eq_subseqs_rev]
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨s, hs, rfl⟩
      rw [mem_subsequences] at hs ⊢
      rw [← List.reverse_sublist_iff]; exact hs
    · intro h
      rw [mem_subsequences] at h
      use l.reverse
      rw [mem_subsequences, List.reverse_sublist_iff]
      exact ⟨h, List.reverse_reverse l⟩

  -- 4. Set of lengths
  let candidates := (subsequences xs).filter isStrictlyIncreasing |>.map List.length
  let post_candidates := allSubseq.filter (List.Pairwise (· < ·)) |>.map List.length
  
  have candidates_eq : candidates = post_candidates := by
    apply List.ext_iff_mem.mpr; intro n
    simp [candidates, post_candidates, mem_post_iff, strict_iff_pairwise]
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨l, ⟨h1, h2⟩, rfl⟩; use l
    · rintro ⟨l, ⟨h1, h2⟩, rfl⟩; use l

  -- 5. Correctness of max
  have zero_mem : 0 ∈ candidates := by
    simp [candidates]; use []
    simp [subsequences, isStrictlyIncreasing]

  let res := candidates.foldl max 0
  
  have max_helper (l : List Nat) (a : Nat) : 
      (l.foldl max a ∈ a :: l) ∧ (∀ x ∈ a :: l, x ≤ l.foldl max a) := by
    induction l generalizing a with
    | nil => simp; exact le_refl _
    | cons y ys ih =>
      simp
      specialize ih (max a y)
      constructor
      · cases ih.1 with
        | head => 
          -- max a y is a or y
          rw [max_def] at h; split at h
          · left; exact h
          · right; left; exact h
        | tail _ h => right; right; exact h
      · intro x hx
        apply ih.2
        cases hx with
        | head => simp; apply le_max_left
        | tail _ h =>
           cases h with
           | head => simp; apply le_max_right
           | tail _ h => simp; right; exact h

  obtain ⟨h_in, h_le⟩ := max_helper candidates 0
  
  simp [longestIncreasingSubseqLength_postcond, longestIncreasingSubseqLength]
  rw [← candidates_eq]
  constructor
  · simp at h_in
    cases h_in
    · rw [← h_in]; exact zero_mem
    · assumption
  · intro x hx
    apply h_le
    simp; right; exact hx
  -- !benchmark @end proof

let lens := (subsequences xs).filter isStrictlyIncreasing |>.map List.length
  let lens_spec := spec_gen.filter (fun l => l.Pairwise (· < ·)) |>.map List.length
  
  have h_set_eq : ∀ n, n ∈ lens ↔ n ∈ lens_spec := by
    intro n; simp [lens, lens_spec, strict_iff_pairwise, mem_subsequences, mem_spec_gen]
    
  have h_res : longestIncreasingSubseqLength xs h_precond = lens.foldl max 0 := rfl
  rw [h_res]
  
  have h_assoc : lens.foldl max 0 = lens.foldr max 0 := List.foldl_assoc
  rw [h_assoc]

  let res := lens.foldr max 0
  have h0 : 0 ∈ lens := by simp [lens, subsequences]; use []; simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]
  
  have h_mem : res ∈ lens := by
    induction lens with
    | nil => contradiction
    | cons y ys ih =>
      simp [List.foldr]; split_ifs
      · exact List.mem_cons_self _ _
      · cases ys
        · simp at h0; subst y; simp
        · right; apply ih; cases h0; subst y; contradiction; assumption

  have h_ge : ∀ n ∈ lens, n ≤ res := by
    intro n hn; rw [←h_assoc]; exact List.le_foldl_max _ _ hn

  constructor
  · rw [←h_set_eq]; exact h_mem
  · rw [List.all_iff_forall]; intro x hx; rw [←h_set_eq] at hx; apply h_ge _ hx

let subs := subsequences xs
  let increasing := subs.filter isStrictlyIncreasing
  let lens := increasing.map List.length
  let result := lens.foldl (fun acc s => max acc s) 0

  have h_incr : ∀ l, isStrictlyIncreasing l ↔ List.Sorted (· < ·) l := by
    intro l; induction l with
    | nil => simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]; exact List.sorted_nil
    | cons x tail ih =>
      cases tail <;> simp [isStrictlyIncreasing, List.sorted_cons, ih]
      exact ⟨fun ⟨h1, h2⟩ => ⟨fun a ha => Int.lt_trans h1 (List.rel_of_sorted_cons h2 ha), h2⟩,
             fun ⟨h1, h2⟩ => ⟨h1 _ (List.mem_cons_self _ _), h2⟩⟩

  have h_subs : ∀ l, l ∈ subs ↔ l <+ xs := by
    induction xs with
    | nil => simp [subsequences]; intro l; rw [List.sublist_nil]; rfl
    | cons x xs ih =>
      intro l; simp [subsequences]; rw [List.mem_append, List.mem_map, List.sublist_cons_iff, ih]
      constructor
      . rintro (h | ⟨s, hs, rfl⟩); left; exact h; right; use s; exact ⟨hs, rfl⟩
      . rintro (h | ⟨s, hs, rfl⟩); left; exact h; right; use s; exact ⟨hs, rfl⟩

  let fold_op := fun (acc : List (List Int)) x => acc ++ acc.map (fun sub => x :: sub)
  have h_post_subs : ∀ l, l ∈ ((xs.foldl fold_op [[]]).map List.reverse) ↔ l <+ xs := by
    let inv (acc : List (List Int)) (p : List Int) := ∀ k, k ∈ acc ↔ ∃ s <+ p, k = s.reverse
    have step : ∀ x p acc, inv acc p → inv (fold_op acc x) (p ++ [x]) := by
      intro x p acc h_inv k; simp [fold_op]; rw [h_inv k]
      constructor
      . rintro (⟨s, hs, rfl⟩ | ⟨_, ⟨s, hs, rfl⟩, rfl⟩)
        . use s; constructor; apply List.Sublist.append_right; exact hs; rfl
        . use s ++ [x]; constructor; rw [List.sublist_append_iff]; use s, []; simp; exact hs; simp
      . rintro ⟨s, hs, rfl⟩
        rw [List.sublist_append_iff] at hs; rcases hs with ⟨s1, s2, rfl, h1, h2⟩
        cases s2 with
        | nil => left; rw [h_inv]; use s1; simp at h2; exact ⟨h1, rfl⟩
        | cons y ys => 
             have : y = x := by cases h2; rfl; rename_i h; cases h
             subst y; right; use s1.reverse; constructor; rw [h_inv]; use s1; simp
    have h_gen : ∀ (ys : List Int) (acc : List (List Int)) (p : List Int), inv acc p → inv (ys.foldl fold_op acc) (p ++ ys) := by
      intro ys; induction ys with
      | nil => intro acc p h; simp; exact h
      | cons z zs ih => intro acc p h; simp; apply ih; apply step _ _ _ h
    have h_final := h_gen xs [[]] [] (by intro k; simp; constructor; intro h; subst h; use []; simp; intro h; rcases h with ⟨s, hs, rfl⟩; cases hs; rfl)
    simp at h_final; intro l; rw [List.mem_map]; constructor
    . rintro ⟨k, hk, rfl⟩; rcases (h_final k).mp hk with ⟨s, hs, rfl⟩; simp; exact hs
    . intro h; use l.reverse; constructor; apply (h_final _).mpr; use l; simp; exact h; simp

  have h_max_fold : ∀ (l : List Nat) (a : Nat), (a ≤ l.foldl max a) ∧ (∀ x ∈ l, x ≤ l.foldl max a) ∧ (l.foldl max a = a ∨ l.foldl max a ∈ l) := by
    intro l; induction l with
    | nil => simp
    | cons y ys ih => 
      intro a; simp; specialize ih (max a y)
      rcases ih with ⟨h1, h2, h3⟩
      refine ⟨le_trans (le_max_left _ _) h1, fun x hx => ?, ?_⟩
      . cases hx; subst x; exact le_trans (le_max_right _ _) h1; apply h2; assumption
      . cases h3; left; assumption; right; assumption

  have h0 : 0 ∈ lens := by simp [lens]; use []; simp [h_subs, h_incr]; apply List.Sublist.slnil
  rcases h_max_fold lens 0 with ⟨_, h_le, h_mem⟩
  
  simp [longestIncreasingSubseqLength_postcond]
  let post_lens := ((xs.foldl fold_op [[]]).map List.reverse).filter (fun l => List.Pairwise (· < ·) l) |>.map List.length
  have h_eq : ∀ n, n ∈ lens ↔ n ∈ post_lens := by
    intro n; simp [lens, post_lens]; constructor
    . rintro ⟨s, ⟨h_mem, h_inc⟩, rfl⟩
      use s; rw [h_incr] at h_inc; rw [h_subs] at h_mem; rw [← h_post_subs] at h_mem
      exact ⟨⟨h_mem, h_inc⟩, rfl⟩
    . rintro ⟨s, ⟨h_mem, h_inc⟩, rfl⟩
      use s; rw [← h_incr] at h_inc; rw [← h_subs] at h_mem; rw [h_post_subs] at h_mem
      exact ⟨⟨h_mem, h_inc⟩, rfl⟩
  
  rw [← List.forall_mem_iff_forall_list_all]
  constructor
  . rw [← h_eq]; cases h_mem; subst result; exact h0; assumption
  . intro x hx; rw [← h_eq] at hx; apply h_le; exact hx
  -- !benchmark @end proof

have ge_res : ∀ x ∈ 0 :: L1, x ≤ res := by
    rw [List.foldl_eq_foldr Nat.max_assoc Nat.max_comm]; 
    generalize 0 = z; induction L1 generalizing z <;> simp [*]
    case cons y ys ih => 
      intro x hx; cases hx; · rw [h]; apply le_trans (Nat.le_max_left _ _) (ih _ (Or.inl rfl))
      · apply ih; right; exact h

  have zero_in : 0 ∈ L1 := by
    rw [List.mem_map]; exists []; constructor
    · rw [List.mem_filter]; simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]; induction xs <;> simp [subsequences, *]; apply List.mem_append_of_mem_left _ ih
    · rfl

  constructor
  · rw [List.contains_def]; apply (List.Perm.mem_iff perm_L).1
    cases mem_res; · rw [h]; assumption; · assumption
  · rw [List.all_iff_forall]; intro x hx
    apply ge_res; right; exact (List.Perm.mem_iff perm_L).2 hx

have h_max_fold (l : List Nat) (a : Nat) : (l.foldl max a) = max a (l.foldr max 0) := by
    induction l generalizing a <;> simp [*, List.foldr]
    rw [max_comm a, max_assoc]; congr 1; rw [max_comm]

  have h_res : res = lens.foldr max 0 := by simp [res, h_max_fold, max_def]; split <;> rfl

  -- Properties of foldr max 0 on Nat lists
  have h_le_max : ∀ x ∈ lens, x ≤ res := by
    intro x hx; rw [h_res]
    induction lens with
    | nil => contradiction
    | cons y ys ih =>
      cases hx with
      | head => apply le_max_left
      | tail h => exact le_trans (ih h) (le_max_right _ _)

  have h_mem_max : res ∈ lens := by
    rw [h_res]
    have h0 : 0 ∈ lens := by 
      simp [lens, subsequences]; apply List.mem_map_of_mem (fun l => l.length); simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]
    induction lens with
    | nil => contradiction
    | cons y ys ih =>
      simp [List.foldr]
      cases (le_total y (ys.foldr max 0)) with
      | inl h => rw [max_eq_right h]; exact Or.inr (ih (Or.inr h0)) -- 0 must be in ys if y < max ys (unless y=0? no, logic holds if 0 in ys)
      -- Wait, if ys is empty, 0 in lens -> y=0.
      -- If 0 in lens, then either y=0 or 0 in ys.
      -- If 0 in ys, ih holds.
      -- If 0 not in ys, then y=0. Then max y (foldr ys) = max 0 0 = 0 = y.
      | inr h => rw [max_eq_left h]; exact Or.inl rfl

  -- Final result
  constructor
  · rw [←h_lens_eq]; exact h_mem_max
  · intro x hx; rw [←h_lens_eq] at hx; exact h_le_max x hx

unfold longestIncreasingSubseqLength longestIncreasingSubseqLength_postcond

  -- 1. Equivalence of Sortedness
  have h_incr : ∀ l : List Int, isStrictlyIncreasing l ↔ l.Pairwise (· < ·) := by
    intro l; induction l with
    | nil => simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]
    | cons x xs ih =>
      cases xs with
      | nil => simp [isStrictlyIncreasing]
      | cons y ys =>
        simp [isStrictlyIncreasing, List.pairwise_cons, ih]
        constructor
        · intro ⟨hxy, hpair⟩; refine ⟨fun z hz => ?_, hpair⟩
          cases hz with | head => exact hxy | tail _ h => exact Int.lt_trans hxy (hpair.1 _ h)
        · intro ⟨hall, hpair⟩; exact ⟨hall y (List.mem_cons_self _ _), hpair⟩

  -- 2. Equivalence of Subsequences
  have h_subs : ∀ {ys} l, l ∈ subsequences ys ↔ l <+ ys := by
    intro ys; induction ys <;> simp [subsequences, List.sublist_cons_iff, *]
    intro l; constructor
    · rintro (h|⟨a,h,rfl⟩); exact Or.inl h; exact Or.inr ⟨a,h,rfl⟩
    · rintro (h|⟨a,h,rfl⟩); exact Or.inl h; exact Or.inr ⟨a,h,rfl⟩

  let fold_op := fun (acc : List (List Int)) x => acc ++ acc.map (fun sub => x :: sub)
  have h_fold : xs.foldl fold_op [[]] = subsequences xs.reverse := by
    induction xs using List.reverseRecOn with
    | nil => simp [subsequences]
    | append_cons ys y ih => simp [List.foldl_append, ih, fold_op, List.reverse_append, subsequences]

  have h_all_iff : ∀ l, l ∈ (xs.foldl fold_op [[]]).map List.reverse ↔ l <+ xs := by
    intro l; rw [h_fold]; simp only [List.mem_map, List.mem_reverse]
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨a, ha, rfl⟩; rw [h_subs] at ha; rw [←List.reverse_sublist_iff]; exact ha
    · intro h; use l.reverse; rw [h_subs, List.reverse_sublist_iff]; exact ⟨h, rfl⟩

  -- 3. Equivalence of Length Sets
  let L_impl := (subsequences xs).filter isStrictlyIncreasing |>.map List.length
  let L_spec := ((xs.foldl fold_op [[]]).map List.reverse).filter (List.Pairwise (· < ·)) |>.map List.length

  have h_sets : ∀ n, n ∈ L_impl ↔ n ∈ L_spec := by
    intro n; simp [L_impl, L_spec, h_incr, h_subs, h_all_iff]

  -- 4. Properties of Max
  let res := L_impl.foldl max 0
  have h_max_props : ∀ (l : List Nat) (a : Nat), let r := l.foldl max a; r ∈ a :: l ∧ ∀ x ∈ l, x ≤ r := by
    intro l a r; induction l generalizing a with
    | nil => simp
    | cons y ys ih =>
      specialize ih (max a y); simp at ih; refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      · rcases ih.1 with (h|h); simp [h]; apply Or.inl (le_max_left _ _); right; exact h
      · intro z hz; cases hz with
        | head => trans (max a y); apply le_max_right; apply ih.2; left; rfl
        | tail _ h => apply ih.2; right; exact h

  rcases h_max_props L_impl 0 with ⟨h_in, h_le⟩
  have h_zero : 0 ∈ L_impl := by simp [L_impl, h_subs, isStrictlyIncreasing]; use []
  replace h_in : res ∈ L_impl := by cases h_in; subst res; exact h_zero; assumption
  rw [h_sets] at h_in
  exact ⟨h_in, fun x hx => h_le x ((h_sets x).mpr hx)⟩
  -- !benchmark @end proof
